item.title: Supreme Court upholds state transgender athlete bans in girls' and women's sports
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld state laws from West Virginia and Idaho that restricted participation by transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports.

item.title: LeBron James to leave Lakers, play elsewhere in 24th NBA season
item.description: James, 41, played eight seasons with the Lakers and led the franchise to its 17th NBA championship in the 2019-2020 season.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: U.S. announces new bank alert targeting powerful drug cartel
item.description: Two men and nine companies are accused of being tied to a cartel-linked fuel theft ring intended to evade taxes while generating tens of millions of dollars annually.

item.title: Crews unloading equipment outside MSG, fueling Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding rumors
item.description: Crews were seen unloading covered equipment from trucks into Madison Square Garden on Tuesday, fueling rumors that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce could be holding their wedding there this weekend.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $100,000 high-yield savings account can earn currently
item.description: A high-yield savings account is still a viable option for savers, even those currently looking to home $100,000 now.

item.title: What can debt collectors legally do after a borrower dies?
item.description: The debt collection process doesn't always end after a borrower's death, but certain debts are off-limits.

item.title: U.S.-Iran peace efforts inch along, mired by Israel's fight with Hezbollah
item.description: A long-term U.S.-Iran peace deal may depend on a separate agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Analysts say that presents a problem.

item.title: House sinks Rep. Rashida Tlaib's second war powers resolution on Lebanon
item.description: The House voted Tuesday on an updated version of the measure after Democratic leaders opposed the original language.

item.title: Egg producers reach federal and state settlement after price-fixing probe
item.description: Three U.S. egg producers will be required to provide 53 million eggs to food banks and to pay a $3.3 million financial penalty.

item.title: Tidal to label AI-generated music and ban royalties for AI song streams
item.description: Music streaming service Tidal has launched a new policy that will flag any music detected as being fully-generated by AI. Tony Gervino, executive vice president and editor-in-chief of Tidal, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean says he was diagnosed with depression as he returns to Capitol Hill after absence
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean, a Republican from New Jersey, returned to Capitol Hill Tuesday and said he was diagnosed with depression, which resulted in a long hospital stay. Kean last voted on March 5. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Does credit card debt forgiveness make sense this July?
item.description: Credit card debt forgiveness could be the solution borrowers are looking for this summer. Here's why.

item.title: Progressive Democrats challenge incumbents in Colorado primaries
item.description: Democrats are hoping to nab their next major win in Colorado's primary elections on Tuesday. CBS News' Zak Hudak has more.

item.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean returns to Congress, says he was diagnosed with depression
item.description: The New Jersey congressman missed more than 140 votes since March 5 as those around him declined to give specifics about his medical issue.

item.title: USMNT looks ahead to World Cup knockout match with Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description: The pressure is on for the U.S. men's national soccer team ahead of their first knockout match against Bosnia and Herzegovina. CBS News' Elizabeth Campbell reports.

item.title: How Americans feel about the nation ahead of its 250th birthday
item.description: The number of Americans who say they feel very patriotic has declined, a new CBS News poll shows. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto examines Americans' attitudes about democracy, economic prosperity and more.

item.title: Less than 40% of U.S. households can afford a starter home, study finds
item.description: The typical non-homeowner household earns about $7,000 less than what's needed to buy an entry-level home, according to LendingTree.

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down coordinated campaign spending limits
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down federal limits on the amount of money a political committee can spend in coordination with federal candidates.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumors fuel big bets on prediction markets
item.description: The impending wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has already generated more than $4.5 million in wagers, according to Kalshi.

item.title: NASA embarks on daring mission to save doomed telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: NASA is in a race against time as the Swift Observatory telescope in orbit sinks closer to Earth. CBS News consultant William Harwood explains the $30 million salvage operation.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, striking down Trump's order
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday struck down President Trump's executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship.

item.title: Suspect in killings of 2 Israeli embassy staffers returns to court as DOJ seeks death penalty
item.description: The man accused of killing two Israeli embassy staffers in Washington, D.C., is back in court today. CBS News crime and public safety unit's senior coordinating producer Anna Schecter has more on the case.

item.title: Supreme Court says nation's top copyright official can keep job for now
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday said it won't allow President Trump to remove the nation's top copyright official.

item.title: Supreme Court rules on birthright citizenship, transgender athletes bans and campaign finances
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled to uphold birthright citizenship on Tuesday, thwarting President Trump's attempts to end the constitutional right. Meanwhile, the highest court allowed states to ban transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports, and struck down limits on coordinated campaign spending. CBS News' Jan Crawford has more.

item.title: Explaining why the Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship and "the right to have rights"
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship. Columbia Law School professor Elora Mukherjee and presidential historian Douglas Brinkley join "CBS News 24/7 with more.

item.title: Will a new central air conditioning unit lower your electric bill?
item.description: A new AC system may reduce your energy costs, but the savings depend on more than just the equipment you install.

item.title: Supreme Court takes up challenges to AR-15 bans
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to take up challenges to so-called assault-weapons bans in Cook County, Illinois, and Connecticut.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Poll: America at 250 and some of what Americans think is best about U.S.
item.description: Americans weighed in on what's best about the U.S., its greatest invention, most representative food and more in latest CBS News poll.
